details.body: Add daemon-side forced backend cleanup before treating a cancelled daemon-owned run as resumable. `finish_cancelled_dispatch()` marks the snapshot failed and returns after the shutdown grace timeout without killing or pruning tracked backend process groups (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3001-3073`), while the process backend only falls back to an in-process background reap after cancel timeout (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1670-1680`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:2138-2147`). If a supervisor SIGKILLs the daemon in that window, the background reap dies with it, leaving `active-processes.json` and orphaned backend groups behind; later `run resume` from a Failed/Paused snapshot never executes the stale-backend cleanup path (`src/cli/run.rs:1952-1958`, `src/cli/run.rs:3269-3282`).

Stop swallowing `release_task_lease()` failures on daemon-aborted shutdown paths. After cancelled dispatch, the daemon ignores the result of lease cleanup (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1520-1522`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1556-1559`), and the final shutdown sweep only logs cleanup failures (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3078-3094`). But `release_task_lease()` explicitly returns `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` when it releases the writer lock yet cannot remove the worktree/lease or clear the task reference (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3468-3510`). That reintroduces the contradictory durable state this bead is meant to block while the daemon exits as if cleanup succeeded.

Propagate non-cancellation errors from the aborted-task fast paths in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`. After `drive_dispatch_multi_repo()` / `drive_dispatch()` returns, the code at lines 844-862 and 1545-1548 exits as soon as `latest_task.status == TaskStatus::Aborted`, without inspecting `outcome`. That drops the new errors returned by `finish_cancelled_dispatch()` at lines 3003-3114 (for example, failed backend-process cleanup during daemon shutdown/cancellation), then still releases the task lease and reports a clean abort. Only `Ok(())` / `Err(AppError::InvocationCancelled { .. })` should be swallowed here; any other error needs to surface before the lease is cleared.

Add a daemon-side repair path for interrupted cancellation handoffs that survive only as `run.json`. `prepare_cancelled_dispatch_handoff` eagerly flips the snapshot to Failed and removes `run.pid` before the grace wait ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2894-2913]), but the only code that appends the missing `run_failed` journal event is `finalize_cancelled_dispatch_handoff`, which only runs if the same process survives to finish the grace path ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2916-2980,3031-3098]). In the supervisor-SIGKILL case from the bead description, the daemon can die after persisting the handoff and before finalization, leaving a permanently incomplete journal for that run. Mirror the CLI signal path’s later repair hook (see [src/cli/run.rs:1972-2024,3213-3217]) so a later status/resume/reconcile pass can append the missing terminal event.

Stop discarding `release_task_lease` errors on the remaining daemon terminal paths. `release_task_lease` now returns `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` when worktree/lease cleanup only partially succeeded ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3565-3614]), but the daemon still ignores that result after aborted PR handling, PR-runtime failure, reconciliation failure, normal completion, and ordinary dispatch failure in both multi-repo and single-repo flows ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:941-946,972-977,1033-1056,1087-1088,1595-1600,1628-1635,1651-1652]). Those branches can still report a task as terminal and move on even though the durable lease/worktree state is contradictory. Propagate or quarantine those errors the same way the newly patched aborted/shutdown paths do.

Stop swallowing partial lease-cleanup failures in the single-repo daemon loop. `release_task_lease()` now reports `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` when worktree/lease cleanup only partially succeeds (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3640-3686`), but `process_cycle()` still catches every `process_task()` error, logs it, and continues (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1127-1134`). In single-repo mode `run()` therefore keeps returning success and dispatching later tasks after a terminal cleanup left contradictory durable state (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:235-260`). Bubble this error out of `process_cycle()` or otherwise stop/quarantine the daemon when lease cleanup is partial.

Wire the daemon cancellation-handoff journal repair into the shared status/resume path instead of keeping it daemon-internal. The new repair helper only runs via `load_dispatch_run_snapshot()`, and that loader is only called right before daemon dispatch (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2554-2564`, `2647-2655`, `2908-2921`). `run status`/terminal sync and `run resume` still call the old signal-only repair helper (`src/cli/run.rs:1972-2024`, `1096-1108`, `3212-3217`), whose summary check does not recognize the daemon cancellation summaries. If the daemon dies in the handoff window, those user-facing recovery flows still leave the journal missing its terminal `run_failed` event.

Propagate or persistently quarantine `AppError::LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` in the multi-repo daemon loop as well. The new fail-closed behavior only exists in the single-repo path (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1130-1139`), but `process_task_multi_repo()` still bubbles `release_task_lease()` failures up into `process_cycle_multi_repo()`, where the generic branch at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:589-610` just logs and breaks the current cycle. On the next poll that repo is eligible again even though the prior task kept contradictory durable lease/worktree state, so GitHub/multi-repo daemon deployments still do not actually block on partial cleanup failures.

Preserve detached stale-owner discoverability until the daemon task's `lease_id` is cleared. In `cleanup_detached_project_writer_owner`, the worktree and lease record are deleted before `DaemonTaskService::clear_lease_reference` runs ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:397-417]). Follow-up recovery only discovers detached owners from `list_lease_records` ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:291-320]) and only attempts detached cleanup when `observed_owner` is present ([src/cli/run.rs:1835-1846]). If the task-file write fails, the first `run resume`/`run stop` attempt returns `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure`, but the next attempt no longer sees any detached owner and proceeds while the daemon task still carries a stale `lease_id`. Keep the lease record until the task reference is cleared, or extend detached-owner discovery to fall back to task-side orphaned lease references.

Treat post-release task-metadata write failures as partial lease cleanup failures before deciding whether the daemon can continue. `release_task_lease` returns the raw result of `DaemonTaskService::clear_lease_reference` after `LeaseService::release` has already removed the worktree, writer lock, and lease file ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3611-3644]). The new callers only stop/quarantine on `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:611-618], [src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1152-1159]). If clearing `task.lease_id` fails, single-repo mode just logs and keeps scanning, and multi-repo mode does not mark `label_dirty`, leaving a terminal task with a stale lease reference and no Phase 0 recovery path. Map this branch to `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` or otherwise quarantine it as contradictory durable state.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3636-3677` now removes `run.pid` via `remove_owned_run_pid_file(base_dir, ...)`, but in multi-repo callers `base_dir` is the per-repo daemon state dir, not the checkout root. `FileSystem::read_pid_file` resolves paths from `base_dir/.git` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:901-941`), so multi-repo cleanup looks under `<data-dir>/.../daemon/.git/ralph-burning-live/...` and never touches the real checkout pid file. That leaves stale `run.pid` files behind after daemon task completion/shutdown in multi-repo mode, which is exactly the stale-state regression this bead is trying to eliminate.

The new detached-owner fallback is only wired into failed/paused recovery (`src/cli/run.rs:1952-1955`). Stale-running recovery still uses `observed_running_attempt_owner()` (`src/cli/run.rs:1653-1668`, `1941-1948`, `3498-3508`), which only consults `run.pid` and the writer lock. If the daemon already released those but failed to clear `task.lease_id`, `run resume` or stale `run stop` on a `RunStatus::Running` snapshot will reacquire the recovery lease and continue without surfacing the contradictory task metadata. The partial-cleanup state is still silently ignored on this path.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:401-454` treats every `read_lease()` failure as if the lease file were simply missing and immediately clears the task-side lease reference through `clear_orphaned_task_lease_reference()`. But `read_lease()` also fails on corrupt/wrong-type records (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2347-2354`) and other I/O errors. In those cases the repo should stay quarantined; clearing `task.lease_id` hides an unresolved durable-state problem and allows the repo to continue with a still-broken lease record/worktree state.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:440-451` now treats missing-worktree-lease repair as successful as soon as `clear_orphaned_task_lease_reference()` returns, but `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3673-3695` only removes `run.pid` when the pid record belongs to the current daemon PID and suppresses all pid-file read/remove errors. In the real partial-cleanup case this branch is meant to repair, a previous daemon process can already have removed the lease record and died before clearing task metadata; when a later daemon runs Phase 0, it will clear `task.lease_id` and `label_dirty` but leave the stale daemon-owned `run.pid` behind because `pid_record.pid != std::process::id()`. The same code also reports success if pid-file deletion itself fails. Please make orphaned cleanup treat unresolved pid cleanup as a partial failure and reclaim dead daemon-owned pid records from prior daemon processes instead of only the current PID.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:327-339` collapses "multiple detached owners found" to `None`, and the resume/stop paths in `src/cli/run.rs:1658-1670`, `src/cli/run.rs:1835-1853`, and `src/cli/run.rs:1954-1957` treat `None` as "no stale owner". If a project has both an orphaned task-side lease reference and another stale CLI/worktree lease, `run resume` / `run stop` can reacquire the writer lock and proceed even though contradictory durable cleanup state still exists. Ambiguous detached-owner state needs to surface as a blocking error, not be silently ignored.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3642-3663` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3698-3728` now require `run.pid` to be reclaimable before clearing `task.lease_id`. On the success path, though, `LeaseService::release()` has already released the writer lock and removed the lease record (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:63-77`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:332-422`), so another process can legitimately acquire the project and write a fresh `run.pid` in that gap. In that race, `remove_owned_run_pid_file()` returns `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure`, the finished task keeps its old `lease_id`, and later recovery falsely quarantines a task whose old resources were already fully cleaned up.

Add an attempt-based fallback in daemon cancellation cleanup for cases where `run.pid` is already gone or no longer matches the current daemon process. `prepare_cancelled_dispatch_handoff()` explicitly preserves `expected_attempt` when the pid file is missing/mismatched (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2943-2961`), but both `finish_cancelled_dispatch()` branches only try `reconcile_cancelled_dispatch_run()` when `interrupted_marker_persisted` is false (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3124-3143`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3156-3168`). That helper calls `mark_current_process_running_run_interrupted()` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2877-2900`), which immediately returns `false` if `run.pid` is absent or no longer belongs to this process (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:857-889`). The daemon then surfaces `InvocationCancelled`, callers release the task lease, and the project can be left with a stale `run.json` still marked `Running`; on the grace-completed path it also skips `cleanup_cancelled_dispatch_backend_processes()`, so tracked backend groups can survive. Use the saved `expected_attempt` to reconcile/finalize interrupted state and run backend cleanup even when the pid file has already disappeared.

Handle post-release metadata failures during daemon shutdown cleanup. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3216-3233`, `cleanup_active_leases()` only marks the task `Aborted` and logs the error. If `release_task_lease()` fails after physical cleanup succeeded but before `clear_lease_reference()` completes (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3667-3680`), the task is left with an orphaned `lease_id` while the lease record and `run.pid` are already gone. The only automatic repair path for that state is Phase 0, but it only scans `label_dirty` tasks (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:401-452`), so shutdown cleanup failures are never repaired on the next daemon start. Mark the affected task dirty, or persist an equivalent recovery marker, before returning the partial-cleanup error.

Do not finalize daemon interruption handoffs solely because `run.pid` is missing. The new daemon handoff path removes `run.pid` as soon as it persists the interrupted snapshot (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2933-2997`), but it does not persist any daemon-owned liveness handoff. `repair_missing_interrupted_handoff_run_failed_event()` was expanded to daemon summaries (`src/cli/run.rs:1993-2037`), and `run status` invokes that repair path on every read (`src/cli/run.rs:8334-8347`). During the 5-second daemon grace window, a status/sync/read can therefore append `run_failed` while the old daemon-owned orchestrator is still alive, publishing terminal state out of order. Add a daemon-owned handoff/liveness proof, or suppress repair for daemon-owned interruption summaries until process death is proven.

Fix aborted-task cleanup so a non-cancellation error from finish_cancelled_dispatch() does not strand the task lease. finish_cancelled_dispatch() now returns backend/interrupted-state cleanup errors (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3161-3255), but both aborted-task branches call allow_aborted_dispatch_fast_path() before label sync / release_task_lease() (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:77-82, 912-929, 1620-1623). For a task-local abort, any Io/corrupt cleanup error exits early with the task already marked Aborted, its lease still attached, and in multi-repo mode label_dirty still false, so Phase 0 never revisits it and the project stays blocked until TTL-based reconcile.

Run stale backend-process cleanup when repairing an interrupted daemon handoff after the daemon dies mid-shutdown. repair_missing_interrupted_handoff_run_failed_event() only restores the missing run_failed event (src/cli/run.rs:2051-2101), and both load_dispatch_run_snapshot() and handle_resume() consume that repaired snapshot without calling cleanup_stale_backend_process_groups() (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2918-2931; src/cli/run.rs:3304-3360). If the daemon is SIGKILLed after persisting the handoff but before finish_cancelled_dispatch() reaches backend cleanup, the orphaned backend process group survives and a resumed/retried run can start a second backend alongside it.

Thread the daemon store directory through `finish_cancelled_dispatch`/`prepare_cancelled_dispatch_handoff` and call `persist_daemon_cleanup_handoff` with `base_dir` instead of `workspace_dir` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2678-2705`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2960-3009`). `FsDaemonStore` resolves lease files under `FileSystem::daemon_root(base_dir)` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:936-938`), so the current code writes the handoff into the checkout root in multi-repo mode and never persists liveness proof for the real worktree lease.

Split the repair helper inputs the same way: `load_dispatch_run_snapshot` must pass the daemon store dir to the interrupted-handoff repair, not the repo checkout root (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2944-2956`). The repair path reads the handoff lease through `FsDaemonStore`/`read_project_writer_lease_record` (`src/cli/run.rs:2013-2021`, `src/cli/run.rs:2125-2135`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:108-116`), so multi-repo daemon retries can never append the missing `run_failed` event or prune stale backend records even if the handoff was written correctly.

Update `tests/cli.rs:12722-12859`. The ignored `cli_run_resume_recovers_stale_daemon_owned_running_and_reclaims_worktree_lock` scenario still asserts the pre-`rlm.5` behavior (resume succeeds after reclaiming a stale daemon worktree lease), but the current implementation now intentionally fails closed when worktree/lease cleanup cannot be positively proven. Running the ignored test now fails with `lease cleanup partially failed ...`, so the only CLI end-to-end coverage for stale daemon worktree-owner recovery is stale and contradicts the shipped contract. Replace it with a real-worktree success case, or rewrite it to assert the new partial-cleanup failure behavior.

Add a daemon-side restart/Phase-0 repair pass for persisted cancellation handoffs. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2692-2705` and `2773-2786`, the shutdown path marks the task `Aborted` before entering `finish_cancelled_dispatch()`, but the only code that consumes the persisted daemon cleanup handoff is `load_dispatch_run_snapshot()` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2944-2958`, which is only reached from the dispatch paths after a task is claimed again (`2639` and `2732`). Both cycle scanners only repair `label_dirty` tasks and then process `Pending` tasks (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:407-683` and `1179-1219`), so a daemon that gets SIGKILLed during shutdown can leave an aborted task with a missing `run_failed` event / stale backend cleanup that the daemon never revisits on restart unless a human later runs CLI recovery. The daemon needs its own startup/Phase-0 handoff-finalization path for aborted/failed tasks instead of deferring this repair to future dispatches or manual CLI commands.

Broaden daemon-handoff death proof so it still works after the writer lock has already been released. `daemon_handoff_process_death_proven()` in `src/cli/run.rs:2013-2048` only looks up the `cleanup_handoff` through `read_project_writer_lease_record()`, which requires the writer lock to still exist. If the daemon dies after releasing the writer lock but before deleting the worktree lease, the proof is still durable on the `WorktreeLease.cleanup_handoff` field (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/model.rs:274-286`), but `interrupted_handoff_cleanup_candidate()` then suppresses repair at `src/cli/run.rs:2081-2089`. That leaves the missing `run_failed` event and stale backend-process records stuck precisely in the partial-cleanup window this bead is trying to recover. The repair path should search detached worktree leases (and, if needed, orphaned task-side lease references) for a matching cleanup handoff instead of requiring a live writer lock.

Make recovery-owner detection fail closed when a stale `run.pid` or writer lock coexists with additional detached owners. `find_detached_project_writer_owner()` now surfaces orphaned task lease references and ambiguous detached state in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:292-352`, but `observed_running_attempt_owner()` returns the `run.pid` writer owner immediately at `src/cli/run.rs:1658-1665` and `observed_resume_recovery_owner()` only consults detached-owner discovery when no writer lock exists at `src/cli/run.rs:1972-1976`. `acquire_recovery_writer_guard()` then cleans/reclaims only that single observed owner (`src/cli/run.rs:1853-1870` and `1900-1907`). If a project already has another detached owner from an earlier partial cleanup, `run resume` / `run stop` will still proceed and leave the second orphaned owner behind instead of surfacing `ProjectWriterStateAmbiguous`. Recovery needs to merge pid/lock-owner discovery with detached-owner discovery and block whenever more than one distinct owner is present.
